Task describes a cleaned subset with 5 columns: PatientID (added), Age (from 'age'), Sex (from 'sex'), Cholesterol (from 'chol'), SysBP (from 'trestbps')

Algorithm analysis: minimum score 0 (age<45, female, chol<240, sbp<140), maximum score 5 (age>=60, male, chol>=240, sbp>=140); verified by calculating scores for all 303 Cleveland patients

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the Cleveland Heart Disease dataset from the UCI Machine Learning Repository (https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/heart-disease/processed.cleveland.data), containing 303 patient records with ages ranging from 29-77 years (mean 54.4), 67.8% male. The dataset is described as a cleaned subset with 5 columns: PatientID (added by task), Age, Sex (1=Male, 0=Female), Cholesterol (126-564 mg/dL, mean 246.7), and SysBP (94-200 mm Hg, mean 131.7). The composite risk score algorithm uses verified clinical thresholds: Age>=60 (+2 points), Age 45-59 (+1 point), Male sex (+1 point), Cholesterol>=240 mg/dL (+1 point, verified as NHLBI high-risk threshold), and SysBP>=140 mm Hg (+1 point, verified as hypertension threshold). The algorithm produces scores ranging from 0-5 points.
